File_Description: File-level metadata associated with dataset describing files contained within dataset.
User guide providing details on how to use the data contained in the dataset and detailed descriptions of each file.
Ephemeris array file includes the aircraft positional ephemera used in geolocation calculations.
Binary header file associated with an ephemeris array.
LASer format (las) file contains a point cloud of first returns as derived by the waveform processor.
Geolocation array file comprises geolocation information for the waveforms.
Binary header file associated with a geolocation array.
Waveform observation array file includes sensor timing, mirror angle value and geometries, calculated ranges, dark offsets, and intensities used in the geolocation calculations.
Binary header file associated with an observation array.
Outgoing pulse array comprises a 2D array of the outgoing T0 pulses and is a binary file with a 16-bit integer array of 100 columns and number of laser pulses rows.
Binary header file associated with an outgoing pulse array.
Waveform product boundary file is a Keyhole Markup Language (kml) file containing the coordinates of the linear ring polygon that describes the acquisition boundary (ground area) for the flightline.
Product browse file is a Tag Image Format File (.tif) depicting a rasterized point cloud from the flight.
Return pulse array is a 2D array of the waveform return pulses and is a binary file with a 16-bit integer array of 250 columns and number of laser pulses rows.
Binary header file associated with a return pulse array.
Work order file is an Extensible Markup Language (xml) file containing processing metadata from the NEON waveform digitizer.
Impulse response file is an approximation of the LiDAR system response function estimated from a hard target and is a 16-bit integer array zero-padded to 100 bins.
Binary header file associated with an impulse response array.
Impulse response T0 file is the outgoing pulse corresponding to the return impulse response function and can be used for deconvolution of the true system reponse.
Binary header file associated with an impulse response array.
